denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 04/21/2026
UnitedHealthcare Choice Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100696872          Group #: GRP-1088
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-116077
Claim #: CLM-2026-192665
Date of Service (requested): 04/01/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Ridgeway Physician Services

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination regarding prior authorization. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 04/03/2026 for Prolonged exposure therapy beyond visit cap related to Post-traumatic stress disorder, chronic (F43.12). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Prolonged exposure therapy beyond visit cap
Diagnosis (as submitted): Post-traumatic stress disorder, chronic (F43.12)
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service.
Clinical policy applied: CDG-UM108 under UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Guideline (CDG) and applicable Medical Policy Bulletin

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
This request has been processed under a benefit category that does not provide coverage for the requested service (benefit category classification).
Our review applied the criteria in CDG-UM108 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Annual visit limit for services treating Post-traumatic stress disorder, chronic (F43.12) has been exceeded. Additional visits are not a covered benefit this plan year.

The file does not satisfy utilization-management requirements for visit limit exceeded. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a UnitedHealthcare medical director. Peer-to-peer review must be requested before a formal appeal is initiated for the same authorization decision.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100696872) and reference PA-2026-116077. 

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
Clinical Operations
clinical_context: The 72-year-old member has Post-traumatic stress disorder, chronic (F43.12). The member is still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO. He has been treated under commercial coverage with Prolonged exposure therapy beyond visit cap recommended as the next appropriate step. CAPS-5 score 42; completed 12 sessions with minimal symptom change. Submitted documentation supports medical necessity for Prolonged exposure therapy beyond visit cap in the setting of Post-traumatic stress disorder, chronic (F43.12); the denial letter does not discuss those records or cite the objective findings in the file.
